caveats: a: No industry-specific market-share or pricing-power series was found.
a: Product families vary in standardization and certification burden.
rho: Public data do not isolate recurring revenue for NAICS 335932.
rho: Revenue quality depends heavily on end-market and channel mix.
e: The assignment's establishment count is modeled rather than directly observed here.
e: Plants are not equivalent to independently acquirable firms.
s5: The Census statistic covers responding employer-business owners broadly, not this NAICS.
s5: Multiple owners can correspond to one business.
q: The NAICS category spans dissimilar materials and end markets.
q: Operational compatibility cannot be inferred from product classification alone.
d5: Demand indicators are broader than this six-digit industry.
d5: Nominal construction spending includes price effects.
o: ILO exposure is occupation-level and not specific to this industry.
o: Traditional robotics and process automation are separate from generative-AI displacement.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Independent manufacturers selling repeatable custom, private-label, specified, or regional product programs to electrical distributors, contractors, utilities, industrial users, and OEMs; revenue combines replenishment with project and program orders.
lens.deviation_from_default: Narrowed to independent manufacturers with coherent noncurrent-carrying product programs because captive facilities and undifferentiated commodity catalog businesses do not share an actionable roll-up model.
lens.excluded_activities: Current-carrying wiring devices, ceramic or glass electrical insulators, electrical contracting, distribution-only businesses, installation-only businesses, and captive plants.
lens.included_activities: U.S. manufacturing of noncurrent-carrying wiring devices, including electrical junction, outlet, and switch boxes; conduit and conduit fittings; face plates; and transmission pole or line hardware.

a.bridge: The Census product boundary and BLS subsector production profile are translated into likely niche defensibility from specification, tooling, lead time, and distributor relationships. This can overstate advantage where products are standardized and price-led.
caveats: No industry-specific market-share or pricing-power series was found.
Product families vary in standardization and certification burden.
a.change_evidence: SKU-level gross margins, customer retention, bid win rates, sole-source incidence, and distributor concentration by target would materially change the estimate.
a.rationale: Large branded catalog suppliers constrain differentiation, but regional box, fitting, and line-hardware makers can retain specification, tooling, service, and channel advantages.

d5.bridge: Broader electrical-equipment output, national electricity-load growth, and aggregate construction spending are translated into a five-year demand index for noncurrent-carrying devices. This can overstate growth if infrastructure spending bypasses these products and understate it if grid and data-center projects are unusually hardware intensive.
caveats: Demand indicators are broader than this six-digit industry.
Nominal construction spending includes price effects.
d5.change_evidence: 335932-specific shipments, distributor sell-through, utility capex allocations, construction starts, and product-level price-volume splits would materially change the range.
d5.rationale: Electricity-load and infrastructure growth support the category, but weak current construction conditions and uneven broader-sector output temper the base case.

caveats: The assignment's establishment count is modeled rather than directly observed here.
Plants are not equivalent to independently acquirable firms.
e.change_evidence: A verified establishment roster with ownership, revenue, EBITDA, geography, and transaction willingness would materially change this estimate.
e.rationale: A 40-establishment niche can yield tuck-ins, but specialization, geography, owner willingness, and manufacturing capital intensity narrow the actionable set.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 335932 ($914,125,887) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 335932 ($9,739,164,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1962 (margins.json[33]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Machinery EBITDA/Sales 19.62%); revenue/employee $658,719 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 335932); class <5: 29 firms, avg revenue/firm $603,034 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $118,315 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 19 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,336,895 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,047,099 -> IN $1-10M band; class 10-19: 10 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,608,800 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $904,247 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 21 firms, avg revenue/firm $17,150,143 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,364,858 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 14 firms, avg revenue/firm $87,887,214 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $17,243,471 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 18 firms, avg revenue/firm $443,534,056 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $87,021,382 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 40

caveats: ILO exposure is occupation-level and not specific to this industry.
Traditional robotics and process automation are separate from generative-AI displacement.
o.change_evidence: Plant labor-by-task data, automation roadmaps, realized AI productivity, and customer substitution evidence would materially change this estimate.
o.rationale: Generative AI can improve quoting, order entry, documentation, scheduling, and other clerical workflows, but physical forming, molding, assembly, testing, and handling dominate production value.

caveats: The NAICS category spans dissimilar materials and end markets.
Operational compatibility cannot be inferred from product classification alone.
q.change_evidence: Target-level process maps, certification sets, ERP data, SKU overlap, customer overlap, and plant-capacity studies would materially change the estimate.
q.rationale: Metalworking, molding, quality, purchasing, warehousing, and distributor-channel capabilities can be shared, but material systems, certifications, tooling, and utility versus construction requirements can impede integration.

caveats: Public data do not isolate recurring revenue for NAICS 335932.
Revenue quality depends heavily on end-market and channel mix.
rho.change_evidence: Customer-level reorder cohorts, contract terms, backlog conversion, and recurring versus project revenue by target would materially change the range.
rho.rationale: Distributor replenishment, utility standards, OEM programs, and recurring construction products create repeat demand, while project cycles and commodity substitution limit contractual recurrence.

s5.bridge: The Census share of responding employer-business owners age 55 or older is discounted into a five-year sell-side succession probability for this manufacturing niche. It may overstate supply because age does not imply sale and understate it where capital or succession pressures are greater in manufacturing.
caveats: The Census statistic covers responding employer-business owners broadly, not this NAICS.
Multiple owners can correspond to one business.
s5.change_evidence: Industry-specific owner-age distributions, family-succession plans, broker pipelines, and five-year transaction histories would materially change the estimate.
s5.rationale: Economy-wide owner aging supports succession supply, but the evidence is not specific to 335932 and warrants a wide, discounted range.

principal_driver: Long-lived electrification and utility or construction infrastructure demand combined with repeat distributor and program orders.
principal_weakness: Only a modest number of establishments appear available, and the category spans product families whose operational and customer fit may be poor.

sources.supports: Industry boundary and representative noncurrent-carrying wiring-device products.

sources.supports: Broad employer-business-owner age proxy for succession pressure.

sources.title: U.S. electricity demand growth is accelerating

title: Noncurrent-Carrying Wiring Device Manufacturing
